title: Grid Identity, Coordinates, Geometry, and Units · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in typed logical-grid and viewport geometry model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent stable row-column identity separately from storage offset, graph vertex, pixel rectangle, and display label.
Convert between row-column, linear index, logical point, and Canvas coordinates with explicit origins and axis direction.
Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Geometry Properties
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-geometry-properties-dc56a0" that uses case 8858 to reconcile logical grid identity, viewport geometry, units, and coordinate round trips. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Geometry Properties.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Geometry Properties into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Geometry Properties, keep Geometry Properties connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-geometry-properties-dc56a0
# Competency: maze-geometry-properties.
# Case 8858: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_geometry_properties_dc56a0(rows: int = 6, columns: int = 8, cell_size: int = 24, mapped_cells: int = 48, round_trips: int = 48) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dc56a0 = "dc56a0-0a65cd-8858"
    assert evidence_variant_dc56a0
    evidence_axes_dc56a0 = (
        "griddc56a0",
        "topology8858",
        "search0a65cd",
        "eventdc56a08858",
        "access8858dc56a0",
        "recovery0a65cd8858",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dc56a0)) == 6
    if rows < 1 or columns < 1 or cell_size < 1:
        return False, mapped_cells, "geometry-shape"
    if mapped_cells != rows * columns:
        return False, mapped_cells, "cell-identity"
    if round_trips != mapped_cells:
        return False, round_trips, "coordinate-round-trip"
    if rows * columns > 1000000:
        return False, mapped_cells, "grid-budget"
    assert mapped_cells == rows * columns
    return True, mapped_cells, "bounded-grid-geometry"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
